content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Deferred the SIGTERM handoff’s durable `run_failed` journal append until post-wait finalization so the signal wrapper now writes only the interrupted snapshot and removes `run.pid` during the grace-period handoff, then appends a missing terminal event only if the interrupted attempt still owns the failed snapshot and the journal does not already contain a terminal event for that attempt. Also relaxed stale backend PGID cleanup so an authoritative backend leader that has already exited is treated as a successful no-op when no process-group members remain, while leaderless groups with surviving members are still refused to avoid unsafe PGID-reuse kills. Added regressions for the deferred journal-append path and for stale recovery with an already-exited backend leader.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the signal-wrapper, interrupted-run finalization, and stale backend cleanup code paths to confirm how the early SIGTERM handoff could corrupt journal sequencing and why already-dead backend leaders were blocking stale recovery.
2. [Completed] Refactor interrupted-run reconciliation so the early SIGTERM handoff persists only the failed snapshot and pid cleanup, add attempt-aware terminal-status detection for already-interrupted snapshots, and finalize any missing `run_failed` append only after the engine future finishes or times out.
3. [Completed] Adjust stale backend process-group cleanup to allow authoritative backend records whose leader already exited when no process-group members remain, while preserving the refusal path for unsafe orphaned groups that still have live members.
4. [Completed] Add regression coverage for the deferred SIGTERM journal-append behavior and for stale stop recovery when the persisted backend leader has already exited, then run the targeted regressions and full acceptance-command suite on the formatted tree.

## Validation Evidence

- Targeted regression passed: `cargo test termination_signal_wrapper_reconciles_interrupted_snapshot_when_future_returns_without_cleanup -- --nocapture`.
- Targeted regression passed: `cargo test run_stop_recovers_stale_running_when_persisted_backend_leader_already_exited -- --nocapture`.
- Safety regression passed: `cargo test run_stop_refuses_unsafe_orphaned_backend_process_group_cleanup_after_leader_exit -- --nocapture`.
- Full suite passed on the final formatted tree: `cargo test`.
- Lint suite passed with pre-existing warnings only: `cargo clippy`.
- Formatting check passed after formatting: `cargo fmt --check`.

## Outstanding Risks

- `cargo clippy` still emits pre-existing warnings outside this change set (for example `dead_code`, `ptr_arg`, `large_enum_variant`, `too_many_arguments`, and similar style warnings).
- The Unix backend-process-group path was validated on this Linux host; the shared non-Linux Unix implementation was not exercised end-to-end in this pass.
- The worktree also contains unrelated runtime artifact changes under `.ralph-burning/projects/rlm-4/`; they were left untouched.
